2606.27233.title: Bridging Talk and Thought: Understanding Dialogue Dynamics Across Collaborative Problem-Solving Contexts
2606.27233.paper_summary: We present a conceptual framework for analyzing dialogue in collaborative problem-solving contexts, with an emphasis on the emerging dynamics of human-AI and multi-agent collaboration. As intelligent systems become active agents capable of autonomous reasoning and strategic cooperation, understanding the dialogic interaction during collaborative problem solving is increasingly important for optimizing and evaluating such partnerships. Our framework addresses key limitations in current analytical approaches through a hierarchical two-layer coding scheme that integrates cognitive and non-cognitive problem solving with metacognitive regulatory mechanisms. We demonstrate its effectiveness and generalizability across nine datasets spanning multiple domains, and provide insights into how humans and agents coordinate their knowledge, skills, and efforts to solve complex problems, showing in particular that metacognitive regulation can be an essential discriminator of deeper collaboration.
2606.27233.paper_summary_zh: 我們提出了一個概念框架，用於分析協作問題解決背景中的對話，特別強調人類與人工智慧及多代理協作的動態發展。隨著智能系統成為能夠自主推理和戰略合作的主動代理，理解在協作問題解決過程中的對話互動對於優化和評估這類夥伴關係變得越來越重要。 我們的框架通過一個分層的兩層編碼方案來解決當前分析方法中的關鍵限制，該方案將認知和非認知問題解決與元認知調節機制整合在一起。我們展示了其在跨越多個領域的九個數據集中的有效性和普遍性，並提供了有關人類和代理如何協調他們的知識、技能和努力以解決複雜問題的見解，特別顯示出元認知調節可以是更深層次協作的重要區分因素。
2606.27233.author: Zhengyuan Liu et.al.
2606.27233.authors: Zhengyuan Liu, Stella Xin Yin, Min-Yen Kan, Nancy F. Chen
